label: Argentina follows United States lead by formally exiting the World Health Organization
label_zh: 阿根廷效仿美國，正式退出世界衛生組織

timeline.title: Argentina Formally Exits World Health Organization Following Lead Of United States On National Sovereignty
timeline.title_zh: 阿根廷正式退出世界衛生組織 跟隨美國退出以維護國家主權
timeline.sub_title: The Javier Milei administration finalized its departure on March 17, 2026, marking a decisive shift toward bilateral health diplomacy while criticizing the global body's management of the pandemic.
timeline.sub_title_zh: 哈維爾·米雷伊（Javier Milei）政府於 2026 年 3 月 17 日完成了退出程序，標誌著國家轉向雙邊衛生外交，同時批評該全球組織對疫情的管理。
timeline.summary:
Argentina officially finalized its withdrawal from the World Health Organization on March 17, 2026, completing a year-long exit process initiated by President Javier Milei. Foreign Minister Pablo Quirno confirmed the development via social media, stating that the nation’s departure became legally effective exactly one year after the formal notification was delivered to the United Nations Secretary-General. This [ref:1, 9] move makes Argentina the latest high-profile nation to sever ties with the Geneva-based agency, following the lead of its primary global ally, the United States, which completed its own withdrawal in January 2026 under President Donald Trump.

The withdrawal is rooted in deep ideological opposition to the multilateral framework of the organization. President Milei has been a vocal critic of the body, frequently describing it as a nefarious entity that overstepped its advisory mandate during the COVID-19 pandemic. The [ref:10, 11, 7, 1] Argentine administration has characterized the global health measures of the early 2020s as a massive experiment in social control, arguing that the organization prioritized political interests over rigorous science. By exiting [ref:7, 1] the treaty, the Milei government aims to reclaim what it describes as national health sovereignty, ensuring that future public health decisions are made independently of supranational impositions.

While the exit represents a significant break from international health governance, Argentine officials emphasized that the country is not retreating into total isolation. Foreign [ref:7, 1, 2, 13, 10] Minister Quirno clarified that Argentina intends to pivot toward a decentralized model of health diplomacy, focusing on bilateral agreements and regional forums. A key [ref:2, 13, 1, 10, 7] component of this strategy is the decision to remain a member of the Pan American Health Organization. As PAHO [ref:3] operates with a degree of semi-autonomy and manages the Regional Revolving Funds, Argentina expects to maintain access to coordinated vaccine procurement and essential medical supplies without being subject to the broader mandates of the global organization.

The World Health Organization has expressed deep concern over the departure of two of the Western Hemisphere’s largest economies. Director-General [ref:8] Tedros Adhanom Ghebreyesus previously warned that such withdrawals are based on unfounded claims and ultimately make both the departing nations and the global community less safe from future health threats. Within Argentina [ref:10, 11], the move has also sparked domestic debate. Public health [ref:11] experts and opposition figures have voiced concerns that the exit could weaken the country's capacity for epidemiological surveillance and limit its influence in global technology-transfer agreements, potentially complicating the response to future cross-border health emergencies.

Legal experts noted that because the organization's constitution does not explicitly provide a mechanism for withdrawal, Argentina relied on the 1969 Vienna Convention on the Law of Treaties to formalize its exit. This legal precedent, coupled with the synchronized actions of Washington and Buenos Aires, represents the most significant challenge to the organization's authority since its inception in 1948. As the administration moves forward, the focus will shift to how effectively Argentina can leverage its new bilateral strategy to fill the coordination gaps left by its departure from the global health body.
timeline.summary_zh:
阿根廷於 2026 年 3 月 17 日正式完成了退出世界衛生組織的程序，這標誌著由總統哈維爾·米雷伊（Javier Milei）發起的為期一年的退出過程的完成。外交部長巴勃羅·基爾諾（Pablo Quirno）通過社交媒體證實了這一發展，他表示，該國的退出在向聯合國秘書長發出正式通知一年後正式生效。此舉使阿根廷成為最新一個與日內瓦機構斷絕關係的國家，緊隨其主要全球盟友美國之後，美國已於 2026 年 1 月在唐納德·特朗普（Donald Trump）總統領導下完成了退出。[ref: 1, 9]
此次退出源於對該組織多邊框架的深刻意識形態反對。米雷伊總統一直是該機構的直言不諱的批評者，經常形容它是一個在 COVID-19 大流行期間越權的惡劣實體。阿根廷政府將 2020 年代初的全球衛生措施定性為一場大規模的社會控制實驗，認為該組織將政治利益置於嚴謹科學之上。通過退出條約，米雷伊政府旨在重新奪回其所稱的國家衛生主權，確保未來的公共衛生決策獨立於超國家強加的規定。[ref: 10, 11, 7, 1]
雖然退出代表著與國際衛生治理的重大決裂，但阿根廷官員強調，該國並未走向完全孤立。外交部長基爾諾（Quirno）澄清說，阿根廷打算轉向一種去中心化的衛生外交模式，側重於雙邊協議和區域論壇。這項戰略的一個關鍵組成部分是決定繼續擔任泛美衛生組織（PAHO）的成員。由於 PAHO 在一定程度上獨立運作並管理區域循環基金，阿根廷預計將維持獲得協調疫苗採購和基本醫療用品的管道，而不必受制於該全球組織的更廣泛授權。[ref: 7, 1, 2, 13, 10, 3]
世界衛生組織對西半球兩個最大經濟體的退出表示深切關注。總幹事譚德塞（Tedros Adhanom Ghebreyesus）此前曾警告說，此類退出基於毫無根據的說法，最終會使退出國和全球社會面對未來健康威脅時變得不安全。在阿根廷國內，此舉也引發了爭議。公共衛生專家和反對派人士擔心，退出可能會削弱該國的流行病學監測能力，並限制其在全球技術轉讓協議中的影響力，可能使未來跨境衛生緊急事件的應對變得複雜化。[ref: 8, 10, 11]
法律專家指出，由於該組織的章程並未明確提供退出機制，阿根廷依據 1969 年《維也納條約法公約》來完成其退出程序。這一法律先例，加上華盛頓和布宜諾斯艾利斯的同步行動，構成了自 1948 年該組織成立以來對其權威的最重大挑戰。隨著政府推進，重點將轉向阿根廷如何有效利用其新的雙邊戰略，來填補其退出全球衛生機構後留下的協調空白。[ref: null]

timeline.title: Argentina Formally Finalizes Withdrawal From World Health Organization Following United States Lead and Sovereignty Concerns
timeline.title_zh: 阿根廷正式完成退出世界衛生組織，繼美國之後並擔憂主權問題
timeline.sub_title: President Javier Milei’s administration completes the one-year exit process today, citing pandemic mismanagement and a shift toward bilateral health cooperation while maintaining ties with regional bodies like PAHO.
timeline.sub_title_zh: 總統哈維爾·米雷伊政府今日完成為期一年的退出程序，理由是大流行病管理不善以及轉向雙邊衛生合作，同時維持與泛美衛生組織（PAHO）等區域組織的聯繫。
timeline.summary:
Argentina has officially completed its formal withdrawal from the World Health Organization (WHO) today, marking the end of a one-year notice period and solidifying a major shift in the nation’s international health policy. Foreign Minister Pablo Quirno confirmed the development on Tuesday, stating that the exit is now effective in accordance with the timelines established by international treaties. This [ref:5, 12, 4, 1, 7, 10] move follows the precedent set by the United States, which finalized its own departure from the global health body in January 2026 under the administration of President Donald Trump. 

The [ref:12, 5, 9, 4, 1, 7] Milei administration originally notified the United Nations of its intent to leave the WHO on March 17, 2025. Throughout [ref:1] the transition period, Argentine officials have remained steadfast in their criticism of the organization, particularly regarding its handling of the COVID-19 pandemic. Government representatives have frequently characterized the WHO’s pandemic-era recommendations as being rooted in political interests rather than rigorous science, often describing the agency's global prescriptions as ineffective and overreaching. By exiting [ref:9], Argentina seeks to reclaim what it terms as full sovereignty over its national health policies, moving away from multilateral mandates.

Despite the formal break from the Geneva-based organization, Argentina does not intend to isolate itself entirely from global health networks. Foreign Minister [ref:1, 12, 4, 5] Quirno emphasized that the country will continue to engage in international health cooperation through bilateral agreements and regional frameworks. Crucially [ref:7, 4, 5, 6, 10, 9, 1, 12], Argentina remains a member of the Pan American Health Organization (PAHO). While PAHO [ref:8, 7] serves as the regional arm for the WHO, it operates as a semi-autonomous body with its own independent legal status, allowing Buenos Aires to maintain a level of regional coordination without being subject to the broader WHO constitution. 

The decision has drawn mixed reactions from the international community and health experts. Supporters of the Milei administration view the exit as a necessary step in reducing bureaucratic waste and resisting globalist agendas. Conversely, public health advocates and experts warn that the withdrawal could significantly hamper Argentina’s ability to respond to future cross-border health emergencies. Leaving the WHO [ref:1] framework means the country loses direct access to specialized technical support, global funding channels, and a seat at the table where international health regulations are debated and decided.

As the withdrawal becomes permanent, Argentina joins the United States in a growing group of nations questioning the utility of traditional multilateral institutions. The move reflects the deepening ideological alliance between President Milei and the Trump administration, both of whom have advocated for a 'sovereignty-first' approach to governance. While the Argentine government insists it will continue to comply with essential international health regulations, such as the notification of infectious disease outbreaks, the long-term impact on the nation’s public health infrastructure and its standing in the global scientific community remains a subject of intense debate.
timeline.summary_zh:
阿根廷今日已正式完成退出世界衛生組織（WHO）的程序，標誌著為期一年的通知期結束，並鞏固了該國國際衛生政策的重大轉變。外交部長巴勃羅·奎爾諾週二證實了這一發展，並表示退出已根據國際條約規定的時間表生效。此舉跟隨了美國的先例，美國在其總統唐納·川普政府時期，於 2026 年 1 月完成了退出該全球衛生組織的程序。[ref:5, 12, 4, 1, 7, 10]

米雷伊政府最初於 2025 年 3 月 17 日通知聯合國退出 WHO 的意圖。在過渡期間，阿根廷官員一直堅定地批評該組織，尤其是在處理 COVID-19 大流行病方面。政府代表經常認為 WHO 在大流行病期間的建議植根於政治利益而非嚴謹的科學，並經常將該機構的全球處方描述為無效且越權。通過退出，阿根廷尋求重新掌控其所謂的國家衛生政策的全部主權，遠離多邊授權。[ref:12, 5, 9, 4, 1, 7]

儘管與該日內瓦組織正式斷絕關係，阿根廷無意完全孤立於全球衛生網絡。外交部長奎爾諾強調，該國將通過雙邊協議和區域框架繼續參與國際衛生合作。至關重要的是，阿根廷仍然是泛美衛生組織（PAHO）的成員。雖然 PAHO 作為 WHO 的區域分支機構，但它作為一個半自治機構運作，擁有自己的獨立法律地位，這使得布宜諾斯艾利斯能夠在不受更廣泛的 WHO 章程約束的情況下，維持一定程度的區域協調。[ref:1, 12, 4, 5, 7, 6, 10, 9, 8]

這一決定引發了國際社會和衛生專家的不同反應。米雷伊政府的支持者認為退出是減少官僚浪費和抵制全球主義議程的必要步驟。相反，公共衛生倡導者和專家警告說，退出可能會嚴重阻礙阿根廷應對未來跨境衛生緊急情況的能力。退出 WHO 框架意味著該國失去了直接獲得專業技術支持、全球資金渠道的機會，以及在國際衛生條例得到辯論和決定的會議中的席位。[ref:1]

隨著退出變得永久化，阿根廷加入了越來越多的質疑傳統多邊機構效用的國家行列，與美國並列。此舉反映了米雷伊總統和川普政府之間日益加深的意識形態聯盟，兩人均主張採取「主權優先」的治理方式。雖然阿根廷政府堅稱將繼續遵守必要的國際衛生條例，例如傳染病疫情的通報，但對該國公共衛生基礎設施的長期影響以及其在全球科學界的地位仍然是激烈辯論的主題。
